description_long: This is a mid-rise apartment building with a weathered, distressed exterior, featuring five visible floors and a flat roof with a central rooftop structure. The facade is marked by heavy black and white discoloration, likely from age or environmental exposure, and includes a series of protruding balconies on one side. The windows are arranged in a regular vertical pattern, with some floors having metal railings or grates. The rear and side walls are mostly plain, with the back wall showing some barred windows and a ground-level entrance with green doors. The building sits on a paved lot, with the ground surface showing signs of wear and cracking. The overall appearance suggests a utilitarian, possibly Soviet-era or Brutalist architectural influence.
description: A five-story, weathered apartment building with a flat roof, distressed black-and-white facade, and protruding balconies on one side. The structure features regular window arrangements, metal railings, and sits on a cracked paved lot.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main facade with heavy black-and-white weathering, multiple balconies, and a central vertical column of windows.
description_view_1: Rear view reveals a mostly white, cracked wall with barred windows, a rooftop structure, and a ground-level entrance with green doors.
description_view_2: Side view displays a plain, white, heavily weathered wall with no windows or balconies, emphasizing the building's utilitarian design.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows a gray, featureless wall with visible brick texture and no windows or balconies.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are vertically aligned in regular columns, with each floor having a consistent pattern; some windows are paired with balconies or metal railings, while others are barred or plain.
